# MusicHoarder

> Self-hosted, open-source tool that identifies, enriches, and organizes your entire music library automatically. Uses AcoustID audio fingerprinting, MusicBrainz, and Spotify metadata. MIT-licensed. No hosted tier — you run it yourself.

MusicHoarder ingests a messy folder of audio files (wrong titles, missing albums, cryptic filenames) and turns it into a clean, consistently tagged library. It fingerprints each track, matches against multiple metadata providers, sends uncertain matches to a human review queue, and writes a reorganized copy to a destination directory — leaving the source untouched.

## Key pages

- [Landing page](https://musichoarder.app/): Overview, features, how it works, tech stack.
- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/Jeffreyyvdb/MusicHoarder): Source code, issues, releases.
- [Pricing](https://musichoarder.app/pricing.md): Machine-readable pricing (it's free).

## Features

- AcoustID + Chromaprint audio fingerprinting for filename-independent song identification.
- Multi-provider enrichment: AcoustID, MusicBrainz web, Spotify API, community trackers.
- Spotify library import: liked songs and playlists matched against your local library.
- Automatic file copy, rename, and retag pipeline (source → destination).
- Synced lyrics fetching.
- Duplicate detection.
- Manual review queue for low-confidence matches.
- Docker-first deployment with .NET Aspire orchestration and PostgreSQL.

## Tech stack

- Backend: ASP.NET Core (.NET) minimal API with EF Core + PostgreSQL.
- Frontend: SvelteKit 2 + Svelte 5 (runes) + Bun.
- Fingerprinting: Chromaprint / fpcalc.
- Orchestration: .NET Aspire.
- License: MIT.
